Leak Date: Kendrick Lamar is an EP by American rapper Kendrick Lamar that was released as a free digital download on December 31, 2009 by Top Dawg Entertainment. It is his first project to be released under his birth name, previously releasing music under the moniker K.Dot, from 2006 to mid 2009. Executive produced by DudeDawg, dee:jay:dave and Sounwave.

Notes (Join the Discord for updates and:

Leak Date: Section.80 is Kendrick Lamar’s first full-length project, released on July 2, 2011, via Top Dawg Entertainment; although the project was marketed and distributed like a studio album, Lamar has consistently referred to it as a mixtape. The title, Section.80, is a combination of Section 8 Housing and the decade that Kendrick was born in—the 1980s. The subject matter ranges from Black people’s strife due to the missteps of the Reagan Administration, women’s issues, drug abuse, and conspiracy theories about the government; all of this without sacrificing precise, agile lyricism. “The Spiteful Chant” was notably removed from streaming services in August 2019. (While the issue was never discovered, it’s presumed to be because of sample clearance issues.)

(good kid, m.A.A.d city: a short film by Kendrick Lamar)
Notes (Join the Discord for updates and:
(2009) (Before Dr. Dre contributions there was already a whole finished version of a good kid, m.A.A.d city project that MixedByAli says "will never see the light of day.")
(July 2nd, 2011) (Section.80 is released)
June 26th, 2012) (The album is officially titled 'good kid, m.A.A.d. city')
(October 22nd, 2012) (good kid, m.A.A.d city is released)
Leak Date: good kid, m.A.A.d city is Kendrick Lamar’s major label debut, released on October 22, 2012 through Top Dawg Entertainment, Aftermath Entertainment and Interscope Records. Subtitled “A Short Film by Kendrick Lamar,” this concept album depicts a day in the life of a 17-year-old Lamar as he rides around Compton in his mom’s minivan. The first twelve tracks make up the standard edition of the album, and there are a variety of different deluxe editions including various bonus tracks. The album was re-released on March 19, 2013 with a bonus remix of “Bitch, Don’t Kill My Vibe” featuring JAY-Z.

Leak Date: To Pimp a Butterfly is Kendrick Lamar’s second major label studio album, and third full-length project. It was released via Top Dawg Entertainment, Aftermath Entertainment, and Interscope Records. It followed his critically-acclaimed 2012 debut, good kid, m.A.A.d city. The album was very well-received upon release, eventually earning 11 Grammy nominations, and it has gone on to be hailed as one of the greatest hip-hop albums of all time. In contrast to Lamar’s previous projects, TPAB took more heavily from jazz, soul, and funk influences, with such contributors as Thundercat, Terrace Martin, Kamasi Washington, and Flying Lotus providing instrumentation and production.

Name (Check out the Tracker website!):
DAMN.
(What Happens On Earth, Stays On Earth., ain't nobody praying for me, GOD DAMN)
Notes (Join the Discord for updates and:
(March 15th, 2015) (To Pimp A Butterfly is released)
(March 4th, 2016) (untitled unmastered. is released)
(April 14th, 2017) (DAMN. is released)
Leak Date: DAMN., Kendrick Lamar’s fourth studio album, was expected to be released on April 7, 2017, after he rapped these lines on “The Heart Part 4”: "Y'all got ‘til April the 7th to get y'all shit together". However, when the pre-order was released, it was announced that it would appear on Good Friday—April 14, 2017. This is Kendrick’s first full-length project since 2015’s To Pimp A Butterfly. In between the albums, Kendrick released the 8-track EP untitled unmastered. On February 27, 2016, Mike WiLL Made-It teased that he was in the studio with Kendrick on Instagram. A collaboration was reinforced when a HipHopDX staff member stated that a song titled “DNA” produced by Mike WiLL Made-It would appear on the album.

Leak Date: Black Panther: The Album is a compilation of music from and inspired by the 2018 Marvel film Black Panther. The compilation was released on February 9, 2018 (one week before the release of the film) through Top Dawg Entertainment, Aftermath Entertainment and Interscope Records. “All The Stars”, “Opps” and “Pray For Me” all make appearances in the film itself, while the rest are inspired by the movie. The soundtrack is executive produced by Kendrick Lamar and TDE CEO Anthony “Top Dawg” Tiffith.

Name (Check out the Tracker website!):
Everybody Sensitive [V1]
(An Audio Book By Kendrick L. Duckworth, Mr. Morale & The Big Steppers)
Notes (Join the Discord for updates and:
(Feburary 9th, 2018) (Black Panther: The Album is released)
(March 15th, 2020) (COVID-19 lockdowns occur)
Leak Date: After Black Panther: The Album, Kendrick works on his next album with much anticipation, titled Everybody Sensitive. He planned for the album to be more personal. Things were going smoothly with a 2019 release date in mind but Kendrick was not able to finish the album by then, continuing his work into 2020. It's assumed that Kendrick would've used pgLang's YouTube channel as a way to promote his and Baby Keem's next album due to the now deleted "Visual Mission Statement" video. Three known tracklists of the album exists, two from 2018 and a finalized version in 2019, which include notes from Kendrick about the concept, sound, producers, and samples.

Name (Check out the Tracker website!):
Mr. Morale [V2]
(Everybody Sensitive, Mr. Morale & The Big Steppers)
Notes (Join the Discord for updates and:
(Mar 15, 2020) (COVID-19 lockdowns occur)
(Aug 2, 2021) (Kendrick works on the final version of Mr. Morale)
Leak Date: Despite Kendrick's own delays and worldwide COVID lockdowns, he continued to work on the album at a steady pace. Songs received major reworks and by May, a 2020 version of the album was finished in the process of being mixed and mastered. However, Kendrick's team would delay finishing the rest of the album after May 5th since they agreed that COVID was going to delay future plans anyway. Kendrick worked on the album again around July-August 2020 to record the "N95" MV and the tap dancing, but the original sounds of the album started changing by late 2021.
